calendar.matters:
Motion To Strike And Tax Plaintiff Jun Takeda S Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Josh P. Davis, Esq. of HEATH & YUEN, APC, T:415.622.7004, for defendant/moving party, on Court Call;
John P. Strouss, III, Esq. of T: for plaintiff/opposing party on Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS S.F. TOWN TAXI, INC. and ANDREY BLIZENKO's Motion To Strike And Tax Plaintiff Jun Takeda's Memorandum Of Costs, as follows:

Defendants S.F. Town Taxi, Inc. and Andrey Blizenko's motion to strike and tax plaintiff Jun Takeda's memorandum of costs is denied. Defendants rely on CCP 1033(b)(1), which gives me discretion to allow or deny costs when a plaintiff recovers less than the small claims jurisdictional amount. Blizenko allegedly opened his cab door into traffic, sending Takeda flying over his bicycle's handlebars. Blizenko then allegedly fled the scene before police arrived. A reasonable lawyer could value such a case above $10,000 (an arbitrator awarded $7,500) and believe small claims court inappropriate.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Case To Limited Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Oct-31-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT S.F. TOWN TAXI, INC., ANDREY BLIZENKO's Motion To Reclassify Case To Limited Jurisdiction.

Grant. No opposition filed.

The Court will sign the proposed order included with the moving papers.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
